questions.q: Whom did Urmila marry?
questions.explanation: Urmila was the wife of Lakshmana, Rama's devoted younger brother.

questions.q: Who was Urmila's father?
questions.explanation: Urmila was a daughter of King Janaka of Mithila.

questions.q: Urmila was the sister of which famous heroine of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Urmila was Sita's sister, both daughters in Janaka's household.

questions.q: In which kingdom was Urmila born?
questions.explanation: Urmila was born in Mithila, the kingdom ruled by King Janaka.

questions.q: When her husband went to the forest, where did Urmila remain?
questions.explanation: Urmila stayed behind in Ayodhya throughout the long fourteen-year exile.

questions.q: Urmila's marriage was part of a joint wedding of how many of Dasharatha's sons?
questions.explanation: All four sons of Dasharatha were married together at Mithila.

questions.q: Urmila is best remembered for her great act of what?
questions.explanation: Urmila is honored for her quiet sacrifice of separation during the exile.

questions.q: How many years did Urmila spend separated from her husband?
questions.explanation: The exile lasted fourteen years, the entire span of Urmila's separation.

questions.q: To which royal family did Urmila belong by marriage?
questions.explanation: By marrying Lakshmana, Urmila joined the royal house of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Urmila's husband Lakshmana accompanied which brother into exile?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana followed his elder brother Rama into the forest exile.

questions.q: Who was Urmila's mother?
questions.explanation: Sunayana was the chief queen of Janaka and Urmila's mother in tradition.

questions.q: Who was Urmila's mother-in-law in Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana was a son of Sumitra, so Sumitra was Urmila's mother-in-law.

questions.q: According to a famous tradition known as 'Urmila Nidra' (Urmila's sleep), what did Urmila do for fourteen years?
options: She slept on behalf of Lakshmana so he could stay awake
questions.explanation: In the popular tale, Urmila accepted sleep in Lakshmana's place so he could guard Rama and Sita day and night.

questions.q: Which deity is said to have appeared to grant the boon allowing Urmila to take on Lakshmana's sleep?
questions.explanation: Nidra, the goddess of sleep, agreed that Urmila would sleep for both during the exile.

questions.q: Sita and Urmila were married to Rama and Lakshmana; to whom were Janaka's nieces Mandavi and Shrutakirti married?
questions.explanation: Mandavi married Bharata and Shrutakirti married Shatrughna, completing the four weddings.

questions.q: When Lakshmana resolved to follow Rama into exile, what did Urmila do?
options: Released him with her blessing to serve Rama
questions.explanation: Urmila selflessly released Lakshmana to serve Rama, accepting separation without protest.

questions.q: How are Sita and Urmila related as sisters in the Valmiki tradition?
options: They are twins of identical birth
Sita is older and Urmila younger
Urmila is older and Sita younger
questions.explanation: Sita, found in the earth's furrow, is the elder, with Urmila her younger sister.

questions.q: What is the name of the sons born to Urmila and Lakshmana in tradition?
questions.explanation: Urmila and Lakshmana are traditionally said to have two sons, Angada and Chandraketu.

questions.q: Which event led directly to Urmila's long separation from Lakshmana?
options: Kaikeyi's demand sending Rama into exile
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi's boons forced Rama's exile, which Lakshmana joined, causing Urmila's separation.

questions.q: What virtue is Urmila most often praised for in retellings of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Urmila is celebrated for her patient, silent devotion and endurance during the exile.

questions.q: In the wedding at Mithila, the brides Sita and Urmila were given away by which king?
questions.explanation: King Janaka gave his daughters Sita and Urmila in marriage to Rama and Lakshmana.

questions.q: Why is Urmila's role often described as 'overlooked' in the epic?
options: Her quiet sacrifice receives little narrative attention
questions.explanation: Though her sacrifice was profound, Valmiki's narrative gives Urmila little direct attention.

questions.q: Which celebrated Hindi poet wrote a work giving Urmila a central, sympathetic voice?
questions.explanation: Maithili Sharan Gupt's epic poem 'Saket' famously centers Urmila's neglected suffering.

questions.q: In the 'Urmila Nidra' tradition, why did Lakshmana need to remain awake for the entire exile?
options: To guard Rama and Sita without rest
To complete a vow of fasting
questions.explanation: Lakshmana vowed to stay awake guarding Rama and Sita, so Urmila bore his sleep for him.

questions.q: Mandavi and Shrutakirti, who married alongside Urmila, were the daughters of whom?
questions.explanation: Mandavi and Shrutakirti were daughters of Kushadhwaja, Janaka's younger brother.

questions.q: According to tradition, when Lakshmana finally woke from his vow at exile's end, where had Urmila's accumulated sleep returned?
options: It returned to Urmila, who awoke as he did
questions.explanation: When the fourteen years ended, the sleep Urmila had carried left her and she awoke as Lakshmana's vigil ended.

questions.q: Urmila's name is sometimes interpreted to evoke which natural image?
options: A wave or ripple of water
questions.explanation: The name Urmila is associated with 'urmi', meaning a wave or ripple, suggesting flowing emotion.

questions.q: In the family structure of Ayodhya's brides, which pair of wives were sisters by the same parents?
questions.explanation: Sita and Urmila were both daughters of Janaka's household, making them full sisters among the four brides.

questions.q: Which kanda (book) of the Valmiki Ramayana contains the marriage of Urmila and Lakshmana at Mithila?
questions.explanation: The weddings at Mithila, including Urmila's, are narrated in the Bala Kanda, the first book.

questions.q: Modern feminist retellings most often hold up Urmila as a symbol of what?
options: The unsung sacrifice of women whose burdens go unrecorded
questions.explanation: Urmila has become a literary emblem of the silent, unrecorded sacrifices borne by women in the epic.
